mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: feat: add version and icon to opa_windows_amd64.exe
mission.description:
resolve: https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/issues/3171

### What are the changes in this PR?

Using https://github.com/josephspurrier/goversioninfo/ adds a windows icon, product name and current version to the windows exectuable.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cfb9ee0d-bf50-4113-a5ba-91795709e293)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3df27465-3e65-4d0b-9a9b-c55ee534cd84)

### Notes to assist PR review:

run `make ci-go-build-windows` locally to build the executable

resolution.summary:
After merging: https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/pull/7501

Main failed: https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/actions/runs/14380716317/job/40323708024
with the error `$WORK/b001/_pkg_.a(resource.syso): 765938: unknown ARM64 relocation type 3`

This happens because windows is built and then `ci-go-ci-build-linux-static` which doesn't like that `resource.syso` exists in the directory. `resource.syso` gets generated each time anyway so safe to clean it up after a windows build.
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
